ddls:
CREATE TABLE "configuration_registers" (
  "register_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each register */,
  "register_name" TEXT /* Name of the register */,
  "size" INTEGER /* Size of the register in bits */,
  "reset_value" TEXT /* Default reset value of the register */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the register's function */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device this register belongs to */,
  "operational_mode" TEXT /* Current operational mode of the register */,
  "interrupt_enabled" INTEGER /* Indicates if interrupts are enabled for the register */,
  "last_modified_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the register was last modified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("register_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "bit_fields" (
  "bit_field_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each bit field */,
  "register_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the register this bit field belongs to */,
  "bit_position" INTEGER /* Position of the bit within the register */,
  "size" INTEGER /* Size of the bit field in bits */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., R/W, Read-only) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the bit field */,
  "bit_mask" TEXT /* Bitmask for the bit field */,
  "default_value" TEXT /* Default value of the bit field */,
  "last_modified_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the bit field was last modified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("bit_field_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bit_fields_register_id FOREIGN KEY ("register_id") REFERENCES configuration_registers ("register_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_types" (
  "access_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access type */,
  "access_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the access type (e.g., R/W, Read-only) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the access type */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Indicates if the access type is the default for the system */,
  "last_modified_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the access type was last modified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reset_values" (
  "reset_value_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reset value */,
  "register_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the register this reset value belongs to */,
  "value" TEXT /* The reset value to set the register/bit field */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Indicates if the reset value is the default for the system */,
  "last_modified_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the reset value was last modified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reset_value_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reset_values_register_id FOREIGN KEY ("register_id") REFERENCES configuration_registers ("register_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "descriptions" (
  "description_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each description */,
  "register_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the register this description belongs to */,
  "text" TEXT /* The description text detailing the settings */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Indicates if the description is the default for the system */,
  "last_modified_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the description was last modified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("description_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_descriptions_register_id FOREIGN KEY ("register_id") REFERENCES configuration_registers ("register_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "change_logs" (
  "change_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each change log entry */,
  "register_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the register that was changed */,
  "changed_by" TEXT /* User who made the change */,
  "change_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the change was made */,
  "old_value" TEXT /* Old value before the change */,
  "new_value" TEXT /* New value after the change */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the change was made */,
  "change_reason" TEXT /* Brief reason for the change */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the firmware version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("change_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_change_logs_register_id FOREIGN KEY ("register_id") REFERENCES configuration_registers ("register_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_change_logs_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES firmware_versions ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the individual */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, engineer) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "last_login_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status entry */,
  "component_name" TEXT /* Name of the component being monitored */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the component (e.g., OK, Faulty) */,
  "last_checked" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last status check */,
  "component_type" TEXT /* Type of the component */,
  "error_code" TEXT /* Error code associated with the component */,
  "last_modified_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the status was last modified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "firmware_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each firmware version */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device this firmware belongs to */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the firmware */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the firmware version */,
  "firmware_size" INTEGER /* Size of the firmware */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the firmware version is currently active */,
  "last_modified_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the firmware version was last modified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_firmware_versions_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES configuration_registers ("device_id")
)
